axis: atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh HEXA_BIN container-context fix (mirror of bridge-8)
trigger: ω-bridge-8 closure flagged this site (tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh:50 same wrapper-default pattern using $HEXA env name; line-51 -x guard fails loudly inside container instead of producing Exec format error, but latent bug identical)

before.behavior: Host Mac wrapper hardcoded as default. Inside hexa-runner Linux container the wrapper path resolves to host bind-mount but the wrapper would exec the Mach-O hexa.real → Exec format error. Currently pre-empted by line-51 `[ ! -x "$HEXA_BIN" ]` guard that fails loudly with 'hexa runtime not found', because the host wrapper is not necessarily executable from container POV / arch mismatch. Either way: regression script unusable inside container without explicit -e HEXA=/usr/local/bin/hexa override.

after.diff_summary: Replaced single-line default with bridge-8 canonical block (lines ~50-69): introduces _default_hexa_bin, auto-detects container context via /.dockerenv presence OR HEXA_IN_CONTAINER=1 (also fallback heuristic: host wrapper absent && container path present). Selects /usr/local/bin/hexa native ELF dispatcher inside container, host wrapper otherwise. Preserves explicit HEXA env override (legacy variable name kept for backward compat — bridge-8 uses HEXA_BIN, this script uses HEXA, only difference). Comment block mirrored from bridge_health.sh lines 36-44 with ω-bridge-9 attribution.

host.evidence: exit=0, prints 'usage: ... [--layer N] [--shard PATH] [--sample N]' + layer legend. bash -n syntax check also OK. (--help exits before HEXA selection block; selection block validated separately via bash -x trace below.)

container.command: docker exec hexa-exec bash /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh --help; AND docker exec hexa-exec bash -c 'HEXA= bash -x /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh --shard /nonexistent'
container.evidence: (1) --help exits=0 with same usage banner. (2) bash -x trace inside container shows: '_default_hexa_bin=/Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa' → '[ -f /.dockerenv ]' succeeds → '[ -x /usr/local/bin/hexa ]' succeeds → '_default_hexa_bin=/usr/local/bin/hexa' → 'HEXA_BIN=/usr/local/bin/hexa' → '[ ! -x /usr/local/bin/hexa ]' false (no exit). Auto-resolution to native ELF dispatcher confirmed. Downstream 'hexa runtime down' is hexa_runtime_check.sh subordinate complaint, unrelated to bridge-9 fix scope.

other_at_risk_scripts_grep_results.note: Same wrapper-default pattern as bridge_health.sh pre-bridge-8. Designed to be drop-in equivalent of bridge_health.sh — should mirror bridge-8 fix verbatim. Recommended for ω-bridge-10.

Host-wrapper default + uses macOS taskpolicy/jetsam binary (/usr/sbin/taskpolicy) — semantically macOS-only, would not be invoked inside Linux container. Also note legacy $HOME/Dev/ path (not /core/), suggesting stale wrapper location. Low priority.
By design — this script's purpose is to validate the host wrapper exists and routes correctly. Container-context detection would defeat its purpose. Skip.
This is the shared scripts/bin/hexa wrapper itself — uses fallback chain across multiple paths including ~/.hx/bin/hexa. Not a bridge-8 candidate (it is the wrapper, not a wrapper-consumer).
